When I went to New York in 2023, I took a cab everywhere.
This time I've already used the subway twice.
I've never eaten at an American diner.
Last night we walked three blocks and hailed a cab on Lexington.
Let me read that back to you: two blocks north, then left on Lexington. Is that right?
So the total is thirty-two fifty, and the tip is not included. Correct?
I got most of that, but not the number. Was it the 4 train or the 5?
It's very loud in here. Could you say the price again, a little louder?
The fare is thirty-two fifty, plus tolls.
Take the 1 line uptown and get off at 79th Street.
It's three blocks west, on the corner of Amsterdam.
With sales tax, your total comes to twenty-six thirteen.
Hop in. Where are we headed?
The Lucerne Hotel, 79th and Amsterdam, please. Roughly how much is the fare from here?
From here? Thirty, thirty-two, depending on the traffic. It's rush hour, so the West Side is going to be slow.
Sorry, it's loud out here. Let me read that back to you: about thirty-two dollars, and it's slow because of rush hour. Is that right?
That's right. Honestly, if you're in a hurry, the subway is faster. The 1 line runs right up to 79th Street, and it's three blocks from your hotel.
I've never taken the subway here. When I came in 2023, I took a cab everywhere. But this time I've already promised myself I'd figure out the map.
Tonight, take the cab. You've got the bags. Card or cash? And do you want a receipt?
Card, please, and yes, a receipt -- I need it for the company. Is the tip included, or do I add it on the screen?
You add it on the screen. Whatever you think is fair. Most people go fifteen or twenty percent.
Then let's say twenty. Thank you, Marcus -- and tomorrow I'll try the 1 line.
How much is the fare to the Upper West Side?
Could I have the check, please? Is the tip included?
Could I try this on? Where is the fitting room?

Ladies and gentlemen, this is the downtown 6 train to Brooklyn Bridge. The next stop is Grand Central, Forty-Second Street. Transfer is available to the 4, the 5, the 7, and the S shuttle. Please step all the way into the car and stand clear of the closing doors. Due to construction this weekend, the 6 train is running express from Fifty-Ninth Street to Fourteenth Street. If you need a local stop, please take the 4 or the 5 across the platform. It is rush hour, so the next three trains will be crowded, but there is another train directly behind this one. Again, the next stop is Grand Central, Forty-Second Street. Please watch the gap between the train and the platform.

Hi there, welcome in. Sit anywhere you like. My name is Rachel and I will be taking care of you today. Let me run through a couple of things before you order. The breakfast menu runs all day here, so if you want pancakes at four in the afternoon, that is completely fine. Today we have two specials: the soup of the day is tomato, and the sandwich of the day is turkey with avocado, and both of them come with fries or a salad. Now, two things people always ask me. First, the prices on the menu do not include sales tax, so the number on the check will be a little higher than what you read. Second, the tip is not included either, so if you were happy with everything, twenty percent is the normal amount in New York. And if you are in a hurry, just tell me, and I will make it a to-go order. I will give you a minute with the menu.
